content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Fact-Checking Prompts for Factuality Enhancement in Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) often hallucinate or generate non-factual content, especially when prompted with ambiguous or open-ended queries. Current prompting methods lack mechanisms to systematically verify model output for factuality.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly used in high-stakes information retrieval and knowledge-intensive applications, reducing hallucinations is critical for trustworthy deployment. Improving factuality via prompt engineering is a low-cost, scalable intervention compared to retraining or post-hoc filtering.",
  "method": "We propose a two-stage prompting method: (1) The initial user prompt elicits a candidate response from the LLM; (2) an automatically generated meta-prompt instructs the model to fact-check its own answer against a given retrieval corpus or external knowledge source, explicitly flagging unsupported claims. The meta-prompt is dynamically tailored by extracting named entities and factual statements from the initial response, forming targeted verification queries. The final answer is only presented if all claims pass self-verification.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will test on subsets of the TruthfulQA and FEVER datasets. Baselines include vanilla prompting and chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics are factuality (verified/correct claims), hallucination rate, and answer completeness. Human annotators and automated fact-checkers will judge output."
}
